revision.text: #redirect[[en:Shusse_Inari_Shrine_(Kyoto)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社]] [[画像:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|thumb|right|240px|本殿]] '''出世稲荷神社'''（'''しゅっせいなりじんじゃ'''）は、京都市中京区にある神社である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと。稲荷神とも呼ばれる）を祭神とする。
==歴史== 1587年に[[豊臣秀吉]]によって[[聚楽第]]内に勧請された稲荷神社が由来である。1663年に聚楽第から現在の地に移された。「出世稲荷」の名は、立身出世を遂げた秀吉にちなんで付けられたものである。
==その他== [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]の停留所に「出世稲荷前」がある。
{{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=出世稲荷神社| 所在地=京都府京都市上京区千本通旧二条下ル聚楽町| 祭神=[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]| 創建=天正15年（1587年）| 例祭=| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
本殿}} '''出世稲荷神社'''（'''しゅっせいなりじんじゃ'''）は、京都市中京区にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと。稲荷神とも呼ばれる）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== [[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]によって[[聚楽第]]内に勧請された稲荷神社が由来である。[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に聚楽第から現在の地に移された。「出世稲荷」の名は、立身出世を遂げた秀吉にちなんで付けられたもの。 ==その他== [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]の停留所に「出世稲荷前」がある。 [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都市]]

revision.comment: 通り名リンク、カテゴリ変更
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=出世稲荷神社| 所在地=京都府京都市上京区[[千本通]][[二条通|旧二条]]下ル聚楽町| 祭神=[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]| 創建=天正15年（1587年）| 例祭=| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
本殿}} '''出世稲荷神社'''（'''しゅっせいなりじんじゃ'''）は、[[京都市]][[中京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと、稲荷神とも呼ばれる）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== [[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]によって[[聚楽第]]内に勧請された稲荷神社が由来である。[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に聚楽第から現在の地に移された。「出世稲荷」の名は、立身出世を遂げた秀吉にちなんで付けられたもの。 ==アクセス== [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]出世稲荷前バス停下車すぐ。 [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:京都市の神社|しゆつせいなりしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=出世稲荷神社| 所在地=京都府京都市上京区[[千本通]][[二条通|旧二条]]下ル聚楽町| 祭神=[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]| 創建=天正15年（1587年）| 例祭=| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
本殿}} '''出世稲荷神社'''（'''しゅっせいなりじんじゃ'''）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと、稲荷神とも呼ばれる）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== [[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]によって[[聚楽第]]内に勧請された稲荷神社が由来である。[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に聚楽第から現在の地に移された。「出世稲荷」の名は、立身出世を遂げた秀吉にちなんで付けられたもの。 ==アクセス== [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]出世稲荷前バス停下車すぐ。 [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:京都市の神社|しゆつせいなりしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=出世稲荷神社| 所在地=京都府京都市上京区[[千本通]][[二条通|旧二条]]下ル聚楽町| 祭神=[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]| 創建=天正15年（1587年）| 例祭=| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
本殿}} '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けた。以来、出世開運の神として大名・公家の崇敬を受けた。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に現在地に遷座した。 ==アクセス== [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]出世稲荷前バス停下車すぐ。 [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]]

{{神社| 名称=出世稲荷神社| 所在地=京都府京都市上京区[[千本通]][[二条通|旧二条]]下ル聚楽町| 祭神=[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]| 創建=天正15年（1587年）| 例祭=| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
本殿}} '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けた。以来、出世開運の神として大名・公家の崇敬を受けた。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に現在地に遷座した。 ==アクセス== [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]出世稲荷前バス停下車すぐ。[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/index.html] [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社]] [[京都バス|京都バス]]　出世稲荷前バス停下車すぐ。　[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]]

{{神社| 名称=出世稲荷神社| 所在地=京都府京都市上京区[[千本通]][[二条通|旧二条]]下ル聚楽町| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|0|55.461|N|135|44|35.076|E|}}| 祭神=[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]| 創建=天正15年（1587年）| 例祭=[[4月8日]]| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
本殿}} '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けた。以来、出世開運の神として大名・公家の崇敬を受けた。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に現在地に遷座した。 ==アクセス== [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]出世稲荷前バス停下車すぐ。[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/index.html] [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社]] [[京都バス|京都バス]]　出世稲荷前バス停下車すぐ。　[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]]

本殿}} '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けた。以来、出世開運の神として大名・公家の崇敬を受けた。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に現在地に遷座した。平成２４年６月に京都市上京区より左京区に移転 ==アクセス== [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社]] *[[京都市バス]]大原バス停より徒歩10分。[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/index.html] *[[京都バス|京都バス]]　大原バス停より徒歩10分　[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shusse-inari.or.jp/ 出世稲荷神社公式WEBサイト] {{Shinto-stub}}

本殿（移転前）}} '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けたという。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に[[二条城]]西方の[[千本通]]沿い（千本[[旧二条通|旧二条]]付近、京都市上京区千本通竹屋町下ル）に遷座した{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|date=2012-06-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 出世開運の神として大名・公家から庶民までの崇敬を受けた。江戸時代後期には庶民が寄進した300本を超える鳥居が立ち並んでいたという。 近代に入ると千本通界隈が映画興行の街として繁栄したため、[[牧野省三]]や[[尾上松之助]]が鳥居を寄進した{{cite web|url=http://info.kyoto-np.co.jp/odekake/syumatsunavi/080308.html|title=京都いんふぉ　千本通かいわい|publisher=京都新聞|date=2008-03-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。[[京都市電]][[京都市電千本線|千本線]]の最寄りの停留所名には「出世稲荷前」が用いられ、市電廃止後も[[京都市営バス]]のバス停名に引き継がれた{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更　京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 地域に氏子がないこと、文化財指定がないことなどから、老朽化した社殿の維持・修復が困難となり、境内地を売却。2012年（平成24年）6月に京都市[[上京区]]より左京区大原に移転した。 ==アクセス== [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社（移転前）]] *[[京都バス|京都バス]]　大原バス停より徒歩10分　[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] ==脚注== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:大原]]

本殿（移転前）}} [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社（移転前）]] '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けたという。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に[[二条城]]西方の[[千本通]]沿い（千本[[旧二条通|旧二条]]付近、京都市上京区千本通竹屋町下ル）に遷座した{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 出世開運の神として大名・公家から庶民までの崇敬を受けた。江戸時代後期には庶民が寄進した300本を超える鳥居が立ち並んでいたという。 近代に入ると千本通界隈が映画興行の街として繁栄したため、[[牧野省三]]や[[尾上松之助]]が鳥居を寄進した{{cite web|url=http://info.kyoto-np.co.jp/odekake/syumatsunavi/080308.html|title=京都いんふぉ　千本通かいわい|publisher=京都新聞|date=2008-03-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 地域に氏子がないこと、文化財指定がないことなどから、老朽化した社殿の維持・修復が困難となり、境内地を売却。2012年（平成24年）6月に京都市[[上京区]]より左京区大原に移転した。 === 電停名・バス停留所名 === 移転前、上京区に所在していた頃は、[[京都市電]][[京都市電千本線|千本線]]の最寄りの停留所名に「出世稲荷前」が用いられ、市電廃止後も[[京都市営バス]]のバス停名として引き継がれていた。 左京区への移転後、ダイヤ改正までは「出世稲荷前」の名称が変更されなかったが、2013年春季のダイヤ改正にて[[京都市交通局]]、[[京都バス]]、[[西日本ジェイアールバス]]の各社局は停留所名を「千本旧二条」と改めた{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更　京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 ==アクセス== *[[京都バス|京都バス]]　大原バス停より徒歩10分　[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] ==関連項目== *[[出世稲荷神社]] - 他の同名の神社 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:大原]] [[Category:豊臣秀吉|社]]

本殿（移転前）}} [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社（移転前）]] '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けたという。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に[[二条城]]西方の[[千本通]]沿い（千本[[旧二条通|旧二条]]付近、京都市上京区千本通竹屋町下ル）に遷座した{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 出世開運の神として大名・公家から庶民までの崇敬を受けた。江戸時代後期には庶民が寄進した300本を超える鳥居が立ち並んでいたという。 近代に入ると千本通界隈が映画興行の街として繁栄したため、[[牧野省三]]や[[尾上松之助]]が鳥居を寄進した{{cite web|url=http://info.kyoto-np.co.jp/odekake/syumatsunavi/080308.html|title=京都いんふぉ　千本通かいわい|publisher=京都新聞|date=2008-03-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 地域に氏子がないこと、文化財指定がないことなどから、老朽化した社殿の維持・修復が困難となり、境内地を売却。2012年（平成24年）6月に京都市[[上京区]]より左京区大原に移転した。 === 電停名・バス停留所名 === 移転前、上京区に所在していた頃は、[[京都市電]][[京都市電千本線|千本線]]の最寄りの停留所名に「出世稲荷前」が用いられ、市電廃止後も[[京都市営バス]]のバス停名として引き継がれていた。 左京区への移転後、ダイヤ改正までは「出世稲荷前」の名称が変更されなかったが、2013年春季のダイヤ改正にて[[京都市交通局]]、[[京都バス]]、[[西日本ジェイアールバス]]の各社局は停留所名を「千本旧二条」と改めた{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更　京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 ==アクセス== *[[京都バス|京都バス]]　大原バス停より徒歩10分　[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] ==関連項目== *[[出世稲荷神社]] - 他の同名の神社 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:大原]] [[Category:豊臣秀吉|社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/　京都大原　出世稲荷神社]

本殿（移転前）}} [[画像:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社（移転前）]] '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けたという。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に[[二条城]]西方の[[千本通]]沿い（千本[[旧二条通|旧二条]]付近、京都市上京区千本通竹屋町下ル）に遷座した{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 出世開運の神として大名・公家から庶民までの崇敬を受けた。江戸時代後期には庶民が寄進した300本を超える鳥居が立ち並んでいたという。 近代に入ると千本通界隈が映画興行の街として繁栄したため、[[牧野省三]]や[[尾上松之助]]が鳥居を寄進した{{cite web|url=http://info.kyoto-np.co.jp/odekake/syumatsunavi/080308.html|title=京都いんふぉ　千本通かいわい|publisher=京都新聞|date=2008-03-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 地域に氏子がないこと、文化財指定がないことなどから、老朽化した社殿の維持・修復が困難となり、境内地を売却。2012年（平成24年）6月に京都市[[上京区]]より左京区大原に移転した。 === 電停名・バス停留所名 === 移転前、上京区に所在していた頃は、[[京都市電]][[京都市電千本線|千本線]]の最寄りの停留所名に「出世稲荷前」が用いられ、市電廃止後も[[京都市営バス]]のバス停名として引き継がれていた。 左京区への移転後、ダイヤ改正までは「出世稲荷前」の名称が変更されなかったが、2013年春季のダイヤ改正にて[[京都市交通局]]、[[京都バス]]、[[西日本ジェイアールバス]]の各社局は停留所名を「千本旧二条」と改めた{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更　京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 == 祭事 == * １月 　　元旦　歳旦祭　一年の無事平穏を祈るお祭 * ２月 　　　　　　節分祭　厄除け・魔除け・災難除けのお祭　豆まき（福銭入り）・ひいら木授与　出世神楽奉納 * 　　　　　　　　　初午祭　五穀豊穣・商売繁盛のお祭 * ４月　 　 　　　 出世稲荷大祭　広くご神徳を賜るお祭 * ６月 　３０日 　大祓祭　罪・穢れを払うお祭 * １１月 　８日 　火焚祭　秋の収穫に感謝するお祭 * １２月 ３１日　 大祓祭　罪・穢れを払うお祭 * 　　　　　　　　　 除夜祭　無事一年間を過ごし得たことに感謝するお祭 ==アクセス== *[[京都バス|京都バス]]　大原バス停より徒歩10分　[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] ==関連項目== *[[出世稲荷神社]] - 他の同名の神社 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:大原]] [[Category:豊臣秀吉|社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/　京都大原　出世稲荷神社公式サイト]

本殿（移転前）}} [[ファイル:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社（移転前）]] '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けたという。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に[[二条城]]西方の[[千本通]]沿い（千本[[旧二条通|旧二条]]付近、京都市上京区千本通竹屋町下ル）に遷座した{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。この地は[[平安京]][[大内裏]]の大極殿入口にあった、[[応天門]]の跡地と比定されている。 出世開運の神として大名・公家から庶民までの崇敬を受けた。江戸時代後期には庶民が寄進した300本を超える鳥居が立ち並んでいたという。 近代に入ると千本通界隈が映画興行の街として繁栄したため、[[牧野省三]]や[[尾上松之助]]が鳥居を寄進した{{cite web|url=http://info.kyoto-np.co.jp/odekake/syumatsunavi/080308.html|title=京都いんふぉ　千本通かいわい|publisher=京都新聞|date=2008-03-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 地域に氏子がないこと、文化財指定がないことなどから、老朽化した社殿の維持・修復が困難となり、境内地を売却。2012年（平成24年）6月に京都市[[上京区]]より左京区大原に移転した。 === 電停名・バス停留所名 === 移転前、上京区に所在していた頃は、[[京都市電]][[京都市電千本線|千本線]]の最寄りの停留所名に「出世稲荷前」が用いられ、市電廃止後も[[京都市営バス]]のバス停名として引き継がれていた。 左京区への移転後、ダイヤ改正までは「出世稲荷前」の名称が変更されなかったが、2013年春季のダイヤ改正にて[[京都市交通局]]、[[京都バス]]、[[西日本ジェイアールバス]]の各社局は停留所名を「千本旧二条」と改めた{{cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更　京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 == 祭事 == * 1月：元旦　歳旦祭　一年の無事平穏を祈るお祭 * 2月：節分祭　厄除け・魔除け・災難除けのお祭　豆まき（福銭入り）・ひいら木授与　出世神楽奉納 * ：初午祭　五穀豊穣・商売繁盛のお祭 * 4月：出世稲荷大祭　広くご神徳を賜るお祭 * 6月30日：大祓祭　罪・穢れを払うお祭 * 11月8日：火焚祭　秋の収穫に感謝するお祭 * 12月31日：大祓祭　罪・穢れを払うお祭 * ：除夜祭　無事一年間を過ごし得たことに感謝するお祭 ==アクセス== *[[京都バス]]　大原バス停より徒歩10分　[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[出世稲荷神社]] - 他の同名の神社 == 外部リンク == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/　京都大原　出世稲荷神社公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:大原]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] [[Category:豊臣秀吉|社しゆつせいなり]]

{{神社 | 名称=出世稲荷神社 | 所在地=京都府京都市左京区大原来迎院町148 | 位置={{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=出世稲荷神社}} | 祭神=[[宇迦之御魂命]] | 創建=天正15年（1587年） | 例祭=[[4月8日]] | 神事= | 画像=[[ファイル:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
本殿（移転前） }} [[ファイル:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|出世稲荷神社（移転前）]] '''出世稲荷神社'''（しゅっせいなりじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂命]]（[[稲荷神]]）を祭神とする。 ==歴史== 元は[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[聚楽第]]の邸内社として創建されたものである。豊臣秀吉は幼いころから稲荷神を信仰しており、[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）に造営した聚楽第にも稲荷神を勧請した。翌年、[[後陽成天皇]]が聚楽第に行幸し、稲荷社に参拝したときに、立身出世を遂げた秀吉に因んで「出世稲荷」の号を授けたという。聚楽第取り壊しの後も元の場所に鎮座していたが、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に[[二条城]]西方の[[千本通]]沿い（千本[[旧二条通|旧二条]]付近、京都市上京区千本通竹屋町下ル）に遷座した{{wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|date=20120608103127|publisher=京都新聞}}（オリジナル：{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}）。この地は[[平安京]][[大内裏]]の大極殿入口にあった、[[応天門]]の跡地と比定されている。 出世開運の神として大名・公家から庶民までの崇敬を受けた。江戸時代後期には庶民が寄進した300本を超える鳥居が立ち並んでいたという。 近代に入ると千本通界隈が映画興行の街として繁栄したため、[[牧野省三]]や[[尾上松之助]]が鳥居を寄進した{{Cite web|和書|url=http://info.kyoto-np.co.jp/odekake/syumatsunavi/080308.html|title=京都いんふぉ　千本通かいわい|publisher=京都新聞|date=2008-03-08|accessdate=2013-01-30}}。 地域に氏子がないこと、文化財指定がないことなどから、老朽化した社殿の維持・修復が困難となり、境内地を売却。2012年（平成24年）6月に京都市[[上京区]]より左京区大原に移転した。 === 電停名・バス停留所名 === 移転前、上京区に所在していた頃は、[[京都市電]][[京都市電千本線|千本線]]の最寄りの停留所名に「出世稲荷前」が用いられ、市電廃止後も[[京都市営バス]]のバス停名として引き継がれていた。 左京区への移転後、ダイヤ改正までは「出世稲荷前」の名称が変更されなかったが、2013年春季のダイヤ改正にて[[京都市交通局]]、[[京都バス]]、[[西日本ジェイアールバス]]の各社局は停留所名を「[[千本通|千本]][[旧二条通|旧二条]]」と改めた{{wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|date=20130129052407|publisher=京都新聞}}（オリジナル：{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29|accessdate=2013-01-30}}）。 == 祭事 == * 1月：元旦 歳旦祭 一年の無事平穏を祈るお祭 * 2月：節分祭 厄除け・魔除け・災難除けのお祭 豆まき（福銭入り）・ひいら木授与 出世神楽奉納 * ：初午祭　五穀豊穣・商売繁盛のお祭 * 4月：出世稲荷大祭　広くご神徳を賜るお祭 * 6月30日：大祓祭 罪・穢れを払うお祭 * 11月8日：火焚祭 秋の収穫に感謝するお祭 * 12月31日：大祓祭 罪・穢れを払うお祭 * ：除夜祭 無事一年間を過ごし得たことに感謝するお祭 ==アクセス== *[[京都バス]] 大原バス停より徒歩10分[http://www.kyotobus.jp/] ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[出世稲荷神社]] - 他の同名の神社 == 外部リンク == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/ 京都大原 出世稲荷神社公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆつせいなりしんしや きようとし}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:大原]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] [[Category:豊臣秀吉|社しゆつせいなり]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Shusse Inari Shrine | Location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | Deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | Image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|en|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|en|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|en|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|en|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|en|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|en|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop Names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|en|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|en|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|en|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|en|京都バス}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shusse Inari Shrine (disambiguation)|en|出世稲荷神社}} == External Links == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/ Official Website of Shusse Inari Shrine in Kyoto Ōhara] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Ōhara]] [[Category:History of Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo Ward]] [[ja:出世稲荷神社]]

Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|en|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|en|京都バス}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shusse Inari Shrine (disambiguation)|en|出世稲荷神社}} == External Links == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/ Official Website of Shusse Inari Shrine in Kyoto Ōhara] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Ōhara]] [[Category:History of Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] [[ja:出世稲荷神社]]

The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|en|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|千本通}}. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop Names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|en|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|en|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|en|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|en|京都バス}} line. Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|en|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|en|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|en|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|en|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|en|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|en|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shusse Inari Shrine (disambiguation)|en|出世稲荷神社}} == External Links == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/ Official Website of Shusse Inari Shrine in Kyoto Ōhara] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōhara]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:出世稲荷神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Shusse Inari Shrine | Location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | Deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | Image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|en|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|en|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|en|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|en|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|en|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|en|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop Names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|en|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|en|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|en|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|en|京都バス}} line. {{Shrine | Name = Shusse Inari Shrine | Location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | Deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | Image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|en|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|en|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|en|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|en|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|en|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|en|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop Names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|en|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|en|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|en|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|en|京都バス}} line. {{Shrine | Name = Shusse Inari Shrine | Location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | Deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | Image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|en|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|en|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|en|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|en|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|en|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|en|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop Names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|en|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|en|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|en|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|en|京都バス}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shusse Inari Shrine (disambiguation)|en|出世稲荷神社}} == External Links == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/ Official Website of Shusse Inari Shrine in Kyoto Ōhara] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōhara]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:出世稲荷神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | Deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|en|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|en|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|en|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|en|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|en|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|en|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop Names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|en|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|en|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|en|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|en|京都バス}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shusse Inari Shrine (disambiguation)|en|出世稲荷神社}} == External Links == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/ Official Website of Shusse Inari Shrine in Kyoto Ōhara] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōhara]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyō Ward]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo Ward]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:出世稲荷神社]]

A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|ja|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line. {{Shrine | name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|ja|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|ja|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line. {{Shrine | name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|ja|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|ja|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|ja|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|ja|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line. In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|ja|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line. Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|ja|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|ja|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|ja|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|ja|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{coord|35|7|0.6|N|135|49|53|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shusse Inari Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg|250px|]]
Main hall (prior to relocation) }} [[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]] '''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success. == History == The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|ja|聚楽第}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power. After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|ja|二条城}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|ja|千本通}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Ōtenmon|ja|応天門}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} of Heian-kyō's palace. The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Makino Shōzō|ja|牧野省三}} and {{ill|Onoe Matsunosuke|ja|尾上松之助}} to contribute torii gates. Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転　老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}} === Tram and Bus Stop names === When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条}}."{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}} == Festivals and Events == * January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year. == Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス}} line.
