<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11395508]] Wikidata name: Shusse Inari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.11683333|N|135.83138889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}} }}
[[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]]
'''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success.
== History ==
The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] within his [[:en:Jurakudai|Jurakudai]] residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power.
After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of [[:en:Nijō Castle|Nijō Castle]] along [[:en:Senbon Street|Senbon Street]]. This location is believed to have been the site of the [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon]], the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} of Heian-kyō's palace.
The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as [[:en:Shōzō Makino (director)|Makino Shōzō]] and [[:en:Matsunosuke Onoe|Onoe Matsunosuke]] to contribute torii gates.
Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.<ref name="kyoto-np20120608">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転 老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}}</ref>
=== Tram and Bus Stop names ===
When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Tram|1=Kyoto City Tram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375142}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Line|1=Senbon Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375149}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Kyūnijō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref name="kyoto-np20130129">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}}</ref>
== Festivals and Events ==
* January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Bus|1=Kyoto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374813}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/]

[[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]]
'''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success.
== History ==
The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] within his [[:en:Jurakudai|Jurakudai]] residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power.
After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of [[:en:Nijō Castle|Nijō Castle]] along [[:en:Senbon Street|Senbon Street]]. This location is believed to have been the site of the [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon]], the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} of Heian-kyō's palace.
The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as [[:en:Shōzō Makino (director)|Makino Shōzō]] and [[:en:Matsunosuke Onoe|Onoe Matsunosuke]] to contribute torii gates.
Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.<ref name="kyoto-np20120608">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転 老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}}</ref>
=== Tram and Bus Stop names ===
When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Tram|1=Kyoto City Tram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375142}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Line|1=Senbon Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375149}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Kyūnijō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref name="kyoto-np20130129">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}}</ref>
== Festivals and Events ==
* January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Bus|1=Kyoto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374813}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/]

'''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success.
== History ==
The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] within his [[:en:Jurakudai|Jurakudai]] residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power.
After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of [[:en:Nijō Castle|Nijō Castle]] along [[:en:Senbon Street|Senbon Street]]. This location is believed to have been the site of the [[:en:Yingtian Gate|Ōtenmon]], the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} of Heian-kyō's palace.
The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as [[:en:Shōzō Makino (director)|Makino Shōzō]] and [[:en:Matsunosuke Onoe|Onoe Matsunosuke]] to contribute torii gates.
Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.<ref name="kyoto-np20120608">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転 老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}}</ref>
=== Tram and Bus Stop names ===
When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Tram|1=Kyoto City Tram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375142}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Line|1=Senbon Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375149}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Kyūnijō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref name="kyoto-np20130129">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}}</ref>
== Festivals and Events ==
* January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Bus|1=Kyoto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374813}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/]

* January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Bus|1=Kyoto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374813}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11395508]] Wikidata name: Shusse Inari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.11683333|N|135.83138889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}} }}
[[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]]
'''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success.
== History ==
The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|Jurakudai|WD=Q910041|lt=Jurakudai}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power.
After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|Nijō Castle|WD=Q1013399|lt=Nijō Castle}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|Senbon Street|WD=Q11405601|lt=Senbon Street}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Yingtian Gate|en|Yingtian Gate|WD=Q11491257|lt=Ōtenmon}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} of Heian-kyō's palace.
The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Shōzō Makino (director)|en|Shōzō Makino (director)|WD=Q788146|lt=Makino Shōzō}} and {{ill|Matsunosuke Onoe|en|Matsunosuke Onoe|WD=Q1037179|lt=Onoe Matsunosuke}} to contribute torii gates.
Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.<ref name="kyoto-np20120608">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転 老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}}</ref>
=== Tram and Bus Stop names ===
When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Tram|1=Kyoto City Tram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375142}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Line|1=Senbon Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375149}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Kyūnijō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref name="kyoto-np20130129">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}}</ref>
== Festivals and Events ==
* January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Bus|1=Kyoto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374813}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出世稲荷神社 (京都市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shusse Inari Shrine | location = Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City, Sakyo-ku, Ōhara Raigōin-chō 148 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.11683333|N|135.83138889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}} | Established = Tenshō 15 (1587) | AnnualFestival = April 8 | Rituals = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:ShusseinariHonden8772.jpg | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}} }}
[[File:Shusseinari8768.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Shusse Inari Shrine (prior to relocation)]]
'''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success.
== History ==
The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|Jurakudai|WD=Q910041|lt=Jurakudai}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power.
After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|Nijō Castle|WD=Q1013399|lt=Nijō Castle}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|Senbon Street|WD=Q11405601|lt=Senbon Street}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Yingtian Gate|en|Yingtian Gate|WD=Q11491257|lt=Ōtenmon}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} of Heian-kyō's palace.
The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Shōzō Makino (director)|en|Shōzō Makino (director)|WD=Q788146|lt=Makino Shōzō}} and {{ill|Matsunosuke Onoe|en|Matsunosuke Onoe|WD=Q1037179|lt=Onoe Matsunosuke}} to contribute torii gates.
Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.<ref name="kyoto-np20120608">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転 老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}}</ref>
=== Tram and Bus Stop names ===
When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Tram|1=Kyoto City Tram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375142}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Line|1=Senbon Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375149}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Kyūnijō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref name="kyoto-np20130129">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}}</ref>
== Festivals and Events ==
* January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Bus|1=Kyoto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374813}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shusse Inari Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|出世稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusse Inari Shrine (disambiguation)|1=出世稲荷神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395506}}
== External Links == * [http://syusseinari.or.jp/ Official Website of Shusse Inari Shrine in Kyoto Ōhara]

In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Shōzō Makino (director)|en|Shōzō Makino (director)|WD=Q788146|lt=Makino Shōzō|1=Shōzō Makino (director)}} and {{ill|Matsunosuke Onoe|en|Matsunosuke Onoe|WD=Q1037179|lt=Onoe Matsunosuke|1=Matsunosuke Onoe}} to contribute torii gates.
Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.<ref name="kyoto-np20120608">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転 老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}}</ref>
=== Tram and Bus Stop names ===
When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Tram|1=Kyoto City Tram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375142}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Line|1=Q11375149|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375149}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Kyūnijō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref name="kyoto-np20130129">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}}</ref>
== Festivals and Events ==
* January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Bus|1=Kyoto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374813}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/]

'''Shusse Inari Shrine''' (出世稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, in the Sakyo Ward. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, a deity associated with prosperity and success.
== History ==
The shrine was originally established as the private shrine of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} within his {{ill|Jurakudai|en|Jurakudai|WD=Q910041|lt=Jurakudai|1=Jurakudai}} residence. A devout follower of Inari deities since childhood, Hideyoshi enshrined the deity when he built Jurakudai in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15 (1587). In the following year, when Emperor Goyōzei visited Jurakudai and worshipped at the shrine, he bestowed the title "Shusse Inari" (Prosperity Inari), commemorating Hideyoshi's remarkable rise to power.
After the dismantling of Jurakudai, the shrine remained on its original grounds until it was relocated in Kanbun 3 (1663) to the west of {{ill|Nijō Castle|en|Nijō Castle|WD=Q1013399|lt=Nijō Castle|1=Nijō Castle}} along {{ill|Senbon Street|en|Senbon Street|WD=Q11405601|lt=Senbon Street|1=Senbon Street}}. This location is believed to have been the site of the {{ill|Yingtian Gate|en|Yingtian Gate|WD=Q11491257|lt=Ōtenmon|1=Yingtian Gate}}, the main gate to the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} of Heian-kyō's palace.
The shrine gained wide reverence as a deity of success and good fortune, attracting devotion from feudal lords, nobles, and commoners alike. During the late Edo period, over 300 torii gates, donated by worshippers, lined the precincts. In the modern era, the area became a bustling district for cinema, leading notable figures such as {{ill|Shōzō Makino (director)|en|Shōzō Makino (director)|WD=Q788146|lt=Makino Shōzō|1=Shōzō Makino (director)}} and {{ill|Matsunosuke Onoe|en|Matsunosuke Onoe|WD=Q1037179|lt=Onoe Matsunosuke|1=Matsunosuke Onoe}} to contribute torii gates.
Due to challenges in maintaining the aging shrine and its lack of designation as a cultural property, the precincts were sold, and the shrine was relocated in 2012 to Ōhara in Sakyo Ward, Kyoto.<ref name="kyoto-np20120608">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20120608000076|title=出世稲荷、大原へ移転 老朽、資金難で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2012-06-08}}</ref>
=== Tram and Bus Stop names ===
When the shrine was located in Kamigyō Ward, the nearest stop on the {{ill|Kyoto City Tram|ja|京都市電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Tram|1=Kyoto City Tram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375142}} {{ill|Senbon Line|ja|京都市電千本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Line|1=Q11375149|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375149}} was named "Shusse Inari-mae." This name was retained for a Kyoto City Bus stop after the tram's closure. Following the relocation to Sakyo Ward, the stop name remained unchanged until the 2013 spring timetable revision, when it was renamed "{{ill|Senbon Kyūnijō|ja|千本旧二条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbon Kyūnijō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref name="kyoto-np20130129">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/politics/article/20130129000051|title=「千本旧二条」にバス停名変更 京都市交通局、神社移転で|publisher=京都新聞|date=2013-01-29}}</ref>
== Festivals and Events ==
* January: New Year's Day Festival (Saidan-sai) — Prayers for a peaceful year. * February: Setsubun Festival — A ritual for warding off misfortune, including bean throwing and amulets. * Early February: Hatsuuma Festival — Prayers for a bountiful harvest and business prosperity. * April: Shusse Inari Grand Festival — A major festival to receive divine blessings. * June 30: Great Purification Festival — Rituals to cleanse sins and impurities. * November 8: Fire Festival — A thanksgiving ritual for the autumn harvest. * December 31: Great Purification Festival — Year-end cleansing rituals. * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival — A ceremony expressing gratitude for the year.

The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk from the Ōhara Bus Stop on the {{ill|Kyoto Bus|ja|京都バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Bus|1=Kyoto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374813}} line. [http://www.kyotobus.jp/]
